weather_report:
Subiaco settles under a damp hush tonight; the streets are dark-lacquered and the air smells of wet leaves. Moderate rain is due through the small hours, easing to a gentler patter toward dawn, so the morning is likely to begin cloud-heavy and grey. Sunshine will be scarce early; any breaks may not arrive until later, and even then they’ll be tentative. The UV index is negligible overnight and should stay low through the early morning, nudging upward only if the cloud lid loosens by late morning. Expect a softened horizon and muted contrasts, a day more smudged charcoal than bright pastel.

Dress for a cool, damp start: a light waterproof, closed shoes you don’t mind getting speckled, and a small umbrella for the walk between doorways. It’s a good morning to linger over coffee at home, answer a few stray emails, then venture out once the rain slackens for a slow lap of the shops or a quiet library visit. If you’re up before dawn, let the rain be your metronome—stretch, plan the day, keep it unhurried. Later, if the clouds lift a touch, a brief stroll under the plane trees will feel earned; if they don’t, a gallery, a grocer’s run, or a tidy-up at home suits the mood just as well.
